prompt: Edit REFERENCE 1 by PULLING THE CAMERA BACK slightly — the crop is currently too tight. Make the shot a bit looser / less zoomed in so the full hand and vial are comfortably visible with a touch more surrounding scene and hand skin showing, while keeping proportions realistic (vial still only slightly longer than a finger). Keep everything else IDENTICAL: same hand (woman with long white nails), same palm-down top-down POV, same vertical vial orientation, same label facing camera, same outdoor track scene and background, same lighting, same color grade, same 5:4 framing. Only the zoom level changes — wider crop, less tight. Photorealistic, seamless recrop only, crisp focus, no artifacts, label stays exact.

Edit REFERENCE 2 by REPLACING ONLY the vial in it with a Dialed Health 'LIPO-B' vial. Do NOT paste REFERENCE 1 flat — you must RE-LIGHT and RE-RENDER the vial so it belongs naturally in REFERENCE 2's gym scene. The liquid INSIDE the glass vial must be CLEAR and COLORLESS (like water). KEEP from REFERENCE 2: the hand (woman with long white-polished nails), skin tone, nails, grip, top-down overhead camera angle, tight close-up crop, composition, gym scene and background, scene lighting, depth of field, color grade, 5:4 framing. RE-LIGHT the vial to match the gym environment — charcoal/cool ambient cast, overhead directional gym lighting, specular highlights aligned to scene light, scene-integrated shadows on the hand. The vial must look genuinely present in the scene, NOT pasted. LABEL must be EXACT from REFERENCE 1: matte black label, Dialed Health ECG logo on top, product name 'LIPO-B' in clean bold white sans-serif centered below on a single line. No dosing, no extra text. Vial upright and vertical, label facing camera, realistic vial scale (slightly longer than a finger), ONE SINGLE HAND, pristine product with no imperfections. Photorealistic, seamless scene-integrated product with clear colorless liquid, editorial lifestyle quality, crisp focus, no artifacts.
